questions.question: How many such pairs of letters are there in the word 'MONETARILY' each of which has as many letters between them in the word as in the English alphabet in both backward and Forward direction?
questions.explanation: MONETARILY 13151452011891225 The pairs are: ON, OI, NR, NI, EI, TR, TY Hence 7 such pairs.

questions.question: Choose the option that is unrelated (or odd one out amongst) the given set of words.
questions.explanation: All the words except 'leprosy' refer to various procedures carried out on the human body, posthumously or otherwise. 'Leprosy' is a disease.

questions.question: Find the missing term. 19, 21, 44, ?, 538
Choose the option that is unrelated (or odd one out amongst) the given set of numbers.
From the given alternative words, select the one which cannot be formed using the letters of the given word only once SOVEREIGN
questions.explanation: Only the word RAIN cannot be formed by using the given word. Because there is no letter A in the given word.

questions.question: R is richer than any other person, and U is poorer than same number of persons as T is richer than. Q is richer than both P and S. T is richer than U. Who is the poorest person among the given?
questions.explanation: → R is richer than any other person. So, R is the richest one. R > all other given persons. Now, in the question six persons are given as per the hints. → T is richer than U. → U is poorer than same number of persons as T is richer than. So, we get, R > T > _ > _ > U > _ Or, R > _ > T > U > _ > _ It is given that, → Q is richer than both P and S. So, we get, R > T > Q > P/S > U > S/P Or, R > Q > T > U > P/S > P/S Hence, either P or S is the poorest.

questions.question: Arrange the given words in a meaningful order. a) India b) Asia c) Lucknow d) World e) Uttar Pradesh
questions.explanation: The order will be Lucknow< Uttar Pradesh< India< Asia< World

questions.question: In the following question, select the related word from the given alternatives. Perennial : Ganges ∷ non- perennial : ?
questions.explanation: As Ganges is a perennial river, Luni is a non - perennial river.

questions.question: If 120 / 24 @ 17 = 3.4 and 80 / 4 @ 50 = 2.5, then in the expression 64 / 16 @ 10 = M, what is the value of 'M'?
In the following question, select the related letters from the given alternatives. EDKX : MLSF ∷ ACZO : ?
questions.explanation: All the letters are done +8 i.e. E+8=M D+8=L K+8=S X+8=F Similarly, A+8=I C+8=K Z+8=H O+8=W

questions.question: p is to the north of m. m is to the south-west of o and to the west of n. If o is to the east of p then in order to reach n, which direction must o take?
questions.explanation: Since the distance between o and p is unknown, we cannot determine the exact position. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/808745/image038.jpg)

questions.question: In each of the questions below are given some statements followed by two conclusions numbered I and II. You have to take the given statements to be true even if they seem to be at variance from commonly known facts. Read all the conclusions and then decide which of the given conclusions logically follows from the given statements disregarding commonly known facts. Statements: All bears are tigers. All tigers are lions. No lion is a cat. Conclusions: I. No cat is a bear. II. No bear is a tiger.
options: Both Conclusion I and Conclusion II follow
Either Conclusion I or Conclusion II follows
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/clerkimg/2136/image007.jpg) Conclusion I follows.

questions.question: Find the missing number in the given matrix. 61535 77143221 323?667
questions.explanation: The numbers in the matrix are the products of consecutive prime numbers. 2x3=6, 3x5=15, 5x7=35 7x11=77, 11x13=143, 13x17=221 17x19=323, 19x23=437, 23x29=667 Hence the missing number is 437.

questions.question: Find the missing terms. abc _ ea _ _ dd _ bcc _
questions.explanation: abcde/abcdd/abccc In 2nd block, 5th letter has been replaced by 4th letter, in 3rd block, 4th letter has been replaced by 3rd letter and so on.

questions.question: If "-" shows "+","+" shows " x ","x" shows "÷","÷" shows "-" then 8 -3 + 12 - 7 ÷ 8 is equal to
questions.explanation: 8 -3 + 12 - 7 ÷ 8 is changed to 8 + 3 x 12 + 7 - 8 as given in the question. 8 + 3 x 12 + 7 - 8 8+36+7-8 51-8=43.

questions.question: Arrange the following words as per the order in dictionary. 1. Wonder 2. Wizard 3. Wireless 4. Wisdom 5. Winning
questions.explanation: This is the correct form as per the order in dictionary. Winning> Wireless> Wisdom> Wizard> Wonder

questions.question: In a certain language, CREATIVE is written as BBFQWSJD. How can be RELATIVE written in that code?
questions.explanation: In the code, CREATIVE → CREA-TIVE → letters are divided into equal halves and then first half is arranged in alphabetical order while letters in second half are arranged in reverse alphabetical order i.e. ACER-VTIE. Then, ACERVTIE +1-1+1-1+1-1+1-1 BBFQWSJD So, RELATIVE → RELA-TIVE → AELR - VTIE AELRVTIE +1-1+1-1+1-1+1-1 BDMQWSJD

questions.question: A word is represented by only one set of number is given in any one of the alternatives. The sets of number given in the alternatives are represented by two classes of alphabets as in two matrices given below. The column and rows of matrix I are numbered from 0 to 4 and that of matrix II are numbered from 5 to 9.A letter from these matrices can be represented first by its row and next by its column, e.g.; O can be represented by 56, 79, etc and A can be represented by 67, 58, etc. Similarly, identify the set for the word OATH. MATRIX-I 01234 0LMHTN 1MTNHL 2HLTMN 3MNHTL 4NTMLH MATRIX-II 56789 5EOFAG 6FGAOE 7AEFGO 8FAOEG 9OEFGA
questions.explanation: By verifying using 1st option 56 67 33 41 O A T T By 2nd option 87 99 22 86 O A T A By 3rd option 79 58 13 03 O A H T By 4th option 68 75 11 44 O A T H.

questions.question: If a mirror is placed on the line PQ, then which of the answer figure is the correct image of the given figure? ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1856/image027.png) a) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1856/image028.png) b) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1856/image029.png) c) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1856/image030.png) d) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1856/image031.png)
questions.explanation: Option 1 and 3 shows diagonal in the wrong direction. In option 4, square shown in the bottom half of the bigger square is wrong. Four small lines drawn on the corner are wrong. Option 2 is the right answer.

questions.question: A square sheet of paper is folded twice into two halves and then cut as shown in the question. Find how they will look when you unfold it. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1851/image062.png) a) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1851/image063.png) b) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1851/image064.png) c) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1851/image065.png) d) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1851/image066.png)
questions.explanation: On first unfolding, two rectangular cuts will become double in their length. On second unfolding, there will be four rectangles parallel to each other. The semi-circular cut made in the corner will appear at all four corners after both unfolding. Option 2 is wrong as it has only two rectangles and option 3 is wrong as it has two semicircles. Option four has small size of rectangles. Option 1 is the correct option.
